utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format lists 6 items: property, financing, legal_services, moving_services, setup_services, total_budget. Now check the final property solution: it includes all six keys. Next, verify if each is fully specified. 1) property: includes two candidate properties with address, price, details, viewing windows, condo fees, and taxes—sufficiently detailed. 2) financing: includes lender, contact, qualification amount, rate, down payment recommendation, timeline, first-time buyer programs—adequate and tailored to joint financing context. 3) legal_services: named package, cost, and included services—complete. 4) moving_services: service type, cost, inclusions—complete. 5) setup_services: package, setup cost, monthly estimate, inclusions—complete. 6) total_budget: breakdown with ranges for property cost, down payment, itemized costs, totals—complete. Therefore, all 6 items are present and fully specified.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final property solution lists two purchase property options and several service selections. We must match each item in the solution to the ground-truth ratings using exact item names from the ground-truth catalog. The properties correspond to purchase_properties categories: '125 Broadview Avenue, Riverside District' matches 'Move-in Ready' (CAD $320K with 80m² and move-in ready), and '45 Charles Street East, Yorkville District' aligns with 'Modern Apartment' (75m², floor-to-ceiling windows, central). For services: financing uses a general lender, but for mapping we look at explicit named items present in ground truth. The solution explicitly chooses 'Premium Legal Package', 'Standard Moving' (called 'Standard Moving with packing' which aligns with 'Standard Moving'), and 'Premium Utilities Package' (aligns with 'Premium Utilities'). It also includes a 'property_survey' cost of CAD $650, which matches 'Full Structural Survey'. We map only items that have direct name matches in the ground-truth ratings.
